ontology.foodon_gap_note: Specific combination of Mutter Paneer, Hummus, and Lebanese Bread not available as a single FoodOn entry. 'Meal' (FOODON_00002476) is the closest parent category available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: Specific combination of Mutter Paneer, Hummus, and Lebanese Bread not available as a single FoodEx2 entry. 'Mixed dishes' (A0001) is the closest parent category available.

serving.description: 1 set meal (1 serving of Mutter Paneer, 1 serving of Hummus, 2 pcs Lebanese Bread)
serving.natural_language_portion: One set meal, typically weighing 650 grams, provides a complete vegetarian serving of Mutter Paneer, Hummus, and two pieces of Lebanese bread.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central - Mutter Paneer, Hummus, Lebanese Bread (composite estimate)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are a composite estimate based on individual components (Mutter Paneer, Hummus, Lebanese Bread) found in USDA FoodData Central, weighted by their approximate proportions in the 650g serving. Original input values were used as a starting point and validated against USDA data for plausibility.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database (Chickpeas, Green Peas, White Wheat Bread)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 55 estimated based on weighted average of components: chickpeas (GI ~28, low), green peas (GI ~48, low), and white wheat Lebanese bread (GI ~75, high). The composite GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI range (<=55) per University of Sydney GI Database classification. Lebanese bread contributes significantly to the glycemic load of the full serving. Mutter Paneer itself has a low GI due to paneer and peas.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and saturated fat from paneer and cream
hypertension.guidance: While sodium is moderate at 230.8mg/100g, individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt during cooking of Mutter Paneer and less cream to manage both sodium and saturated fat intake within recommended daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate content, especially from Lebanese bread made with refined wheat flour
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chickpeas and green peas offer fibre and protein that moderate glucose absorption, but the Lebanese bread has a moderate-to-high GI; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit bread portions to one piece or request a wholemeal variant to reduce glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and cream in Mutter Paneer
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The meal contains saturated fat (3.85g/100g) from paneer and cream; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request a reduced-cream or yogurt-based Mutter Paneer preparation to lower saturated fat intake and support heart health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices and healthy fats, but nightshade tomatoes present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Turmeric, cumin, coriander, and olive oil in this meal provide anti-inflammatory benefits; however, individuals with RA who are sensitive to nightshades should monitor symptoms due to tomatoes in Mutter Paneer and consider requesting a tomato-free preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: This vegetarian meal set combines Mutter Paneer, a classic North Indian dish featuring soft cubes of cottage cheese (paneer) and green peas simmered in a rich, creamy, spiced tomato-based gravy with turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garam masala, with Hummus, a Middle Eastern dip made from blended chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, and garlic. It is served alongside two pieces of soft Lebanese flatbread (khubz). The Mutter Paneer offers a savory, mildly spicy, and creamy flavor profile, while the Hummus provides a tangy, earthy, and smooth contrast. The Lebanese bread is soft and slightly chewy, ideal for scooping. Nutritionally, the meal provides approximately 161.5 kcal per 100g, with meaningful protein (6.9g) from paneer and chickpeas, moderate fat (10g) including saturated fat from dairy, and carbohydrates (12.3g) primarily from the bread. Dietary fibre (2.3g/100g) from legumes supports gut health and glycemic moderation. The meal is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal. It blends Indian and Middle Eastern culinary traditions and is commonly served as a lunch or dinner set in Singapore's multicultural food scene.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are a composite estimate based on individual components from USDA FoodData Central, as an exact match for this specific meal combination is not available in any approved primary source. GI is also an analogous estimate derived from component GI values. Multilingual names and aliases are derived from common usage and translation.
notes: Potassium_mg, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g were estimated based on typical values for the main ingredients (paneer, chickpeas, peas, wheat bread) from USDA FoodData Central, as detailed data for the combined dish was not available in any approved source. These values are plausible but not from a direct measurement of the exact dish. GI of 55 is at the upper boundary of the low GI classification per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is set to 'low' consistent with the <=55 threshold. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact single-entry code exists for this combined meal.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Populated all mandatory multilingual names; added composite nutrient estimates; added ontology codes; expanded LLM description; added health context conditions.
Iteration 2: Ensured aliases_by_language entries present in flat aliases; refined nutrient_source and gi_source documentation.

Iteration 3: Fixed ontology match_type values from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' to comply with allowed enum ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] (G5 fix). Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to match glycemic_index=55 per University of Sydney <=55 low GI threshold (Q4 fix). Added all aliases_by_language string values to flat aliases array including Chinese, Malay, and Tamil entries that were previously missing (Q8 fix).
